description_long: This is a rustic, stone-and-brick obelisk with a square cross-section and a weathered, tiled roof at the top. The structure is composed of irregularly stacked stones and red bricks, giving it a rough, aged appearance. The central section features a recessed rectangular panel on each face, possibly intended for inscriptions or plaques, though most are blank or faded. The roof is made of overlapping terracotta tiles, some of which are broken or missing, adding to the object's antiquated charm. The base is cylindrical and constructed from a mix of stone and brick, blending into a patch of earth and sparse grass. The overall look suggests it serves as a historical marker or wayfinding monument in an urban or park setting.
description: A weathered stone-and-brick obelisk with a tiled roof and recessed panels, standing on a rough, cylindrical base. The structure has an aged, rustic appearance with visible wear and missing roof tiles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main recessed panel and the most intact section of the tiled roof.
description_view_1: Side view revealing more roof damage and a partially faded panel, with the base blending into the ground.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the tall, narrow structure and the irregular stone-brick pattern, with less visible panel detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing another recessed panel and significant roof damage, with the base clearly visible.
attribute_base_design: The base is cylindrical, constructed from a mix of irregularly shaped stones and bricks, with a rough, uneven surface and no decorative features or steps.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is capped with a four-sided, sloped terracotta-tiled roof, some tiles broken or missing, with no additional decoration.
attribute_surrounding_features: Sparse grass and bare earth immediately surround the base; no other notable features visible.
